run_summary: Portfolio is +0.9% after 4 days (BBY near flat, MANH +1.2%). Both positions entered at low conviction on thin theses (no mechanism stated). BBY re-confirms OVERDONE on identical catalyst with extended 5-day move (27.47%), triggering a trim to lock in modest gains and reduce exposure to a fade. MANH, flagged today as UNDERDONE (conf 3) on a restructuring 8-K, is already in the portfolio at +1.2% with fresh volume/momentum; I'll hold for follow-through rather than average up into unconfirmed guidance. Four new flags arrive: two re-entries (AVAV, BBY) both fail the conf-4+ bar due to lack of new information; BLDR fails for same reason. DKS (RATIONAL conf 3) and PLAB (RATIONAL conf 4) are not buy-eligible per rules (RATIONAL classification). Only MANH qualifies as actionable, but it's already owned and outperforming; no new exploratory positions fit the bar.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-01 with Items 2.05 (restructuring) and 7.01 (Reg FD disclosure)
entries.thesis: MANH filed an Item 2.05 (restructuring/exit costs) plus 7.01 (Reg FD) and the stock is up sharply on above-average volume, extending a multi-day rally. Restructurings filed as standalone events (not bundled with bad earnings) are often read as proactive margin/cost discipline by SaaS investors, especially in supply-chain software where peers have been pressured. The 7.01 disclosure likely contains framing/guidance that the market is digesting positively. If management is reaffirming or raising guidance alongside cost takeouts, follow-through is likely as sell-side updates models.
entries.what_kills: Restructuring is actually a response to weakening bookings/RPO trends disclosed in the 7.01; or the move is purely sympathy with the broader software/AI tape (XLK +2.48%) and fades when sector rotates
entries.reasoning: MANH is already in the portfolio at +1.2% unrealized and the new flag today re-surfaces the identical UNDERDONE catalyst (8-K restructuring + Reg FD, 6/1 filing). While the flag shows strong follow-through (+16.21% 5-day, 1.33x volume), the position is already tracking the thesis. An ADD here would only make sense if confidence improved (new analyst upgrades, confirmed guidance language in the 7.01 exhibit) AND the position still had meaningful room under the 25% single-position limit (currently ~6.1% of bankroll at cost). No fresh information warrants increasing the bet at this moment; HOLD the existing 3.88 shares and wait for confirmatory news (guidance, analyst action) before deciding whether to ADD.

entries.name: Dick's Sporting Goods Inc
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings report 5/27 with separate 8.01 disclosure, compounded by XLY weakness today
entries.thesis: DKS reported on 5/27 (Items 2.02 + 8.01 + 9.01) and the stock is now down meaningfully cumulatively. XLY is -2.22% today, so today's leg is partly sector sympathy on top of an already-soft earnings reaction. The 8.01 catch-all alongside 2.02 sometimes signals a separately disclosed item (Foot Locker integration update is the obvious candidate given recent M&A). Magnitude looks consistent with weak consumer tape + integration overhang — not obviously overdone or underdone.
entries.what_kills: Sharp reversal if the 8.01 disclosure turns out to be benign (e.g., a buyback authorization rather than guidance cut)
entries.reasoning: Flagged as RATIONAL conf 3, not OVERDONE or UNDERDONE. Per the rules, only buy-eligible discoveries with OVERDONE/UNDERDONE classification and confidence >= 3 are actionable. DKS falls outside the buy-eligible pool despite a coherent setup (post-earnings drift on weak consumer tape + 8.01 disclosure). The thesis is sound (Items 2.02 + 8.01 alongside XLY weakness suggests either guide cut or M&A integration overhang), but without an OVERDONE/UNDERDONE classification, it does not clear the entry bar. If the 8.01 disclosure is confirmed to be bearish (e.g., integration charges, guidance cut), a SKIP is appropriate pending re-flag with proper classification.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q2 earnings release (8-K filed 2026-05-28 with items 2.02 + 9.01)
entries.thesis: An ~36% single-day collapse on 8x volume following a 2.02 earnings 8-K, in a small semi-equipment supplier, is the signature of a genuine miss + guide cut, not an overreaction. The fact that the stock was already down ~32% over five days suggests informed selling preceded the print — the magnitude of the gap is consistent with a structural reset (guide cut, customer loss, or margin collapse) rather than a one-quarter blip. Hard to call OVERDONE without seeing the actual numbers; in photomask, customer concentration risk (large foundry/IDM orders) means a single lost program can reset the multiple permanently.
entries.what_kills: If the print shows only a modest miss with no guide change and the move is driven by a single forced seller or panic — then a snapback toward the pre-drawdown level becomes likely and OVERDONE would have been the right call
entries.reasoning: Flagged as RATIONAL conf 4, not OVERDONE or UNDERDONE. Per rules, only buy-eligible discoveries with OVERDONE/UNDERDONE classification qualify for entry. PLAB's -36% single-day collapse on 8.38x volume is extreme, but the flag explicitly avoids calling it overdone, instead noting that the magnitude is consistent with a structural reset (guide cut, customer loss, margin collapse). The thesis is rigorous (photomask customer concentration means a single lost order resets multiples permanently), but without an OVERDONE label, it does not fit the actionable category. A RATIONAL conf 4 on a massive gap is closer to a 'wait for stabilization / more information' setup than a conviction buy. If follow-up data reveals the miss was contained and upside exists, a re-flag as UNDERDONE would be appropriate.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Iran missile launch headlines and sector-wide defense bid (ITA +2.2%)
entries.thesis: AVAV up ~18% today on 3.3x volume with no 8-K, riding Iran missile headlines and broader defense tape (ITA +2.2%). The stock is up ~31% in five days with no company-specific catalyst visible — that's a positioning move, not a fundamentals move. Drone/loitering-munition names get bid hard on Middle East escalation, but historically these spikes retrace 30-50% within two weeks once the headline news fades, absent an actual contract award. The five-day magnitude is now disproportionate to what's been confirmed.
entries.what_kills: An actual large Switchblade or JUMP-20 contract announcement, or material Iran escalation (strikes, Strait closure) — either would justify the move and convert it to UNDERDONE
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning: AVAV was closed 0 days ago for a -5.31% realized loss on OVERDONE conf-3 flag. The new flag today re-surfaces the identical OVERDONE thesis (Iran missile headline + defense sector bid, no company-specific catalyst, 30.83% five-day move on 3.28x volume). To justify a re-buy, two conditions must hold: (1) confidence >= 4, and (2) genuinely NEW information that did not exist at exit. This flag carries confidence 3, which fails bar (1) — the execution layer will downgrade any BUY here to WATCH regardless of my decision. Additionally, the thesis has NOT evolved: the same geopolitical headline, the same defense sector sympathy (ITA +2.2%), no new 8-K or contract award. The exit reasoning explicitly stated the move has overextended past the point where geopolitical upside justifies hold; the new flag confirms that overextension. Return WATCH. If a material Switchblade/JUMP-20 contract award surfaces, or Iran escalation dramatically deepens, re-flag at conf 4 with new catalyst and reassess.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; momentum extension against deteriorating mortgage backdrop
entries.thesis: BLDR has run sharply over the past week and added another leg today, but today's news flow includes mortgage refi demand dropping 18% as rates hit a nine-month high. That is a direct demand headwind for residential building products. The continuation move appears to be momentum-driven rather than fundamentally supported by the rate backdrop. Setup favors mean reversion if rates stay elevated.
entries.what_kills: Tape-level rate reversal lower; specific M&A or capital-return announcement from BLDR; homebuilder peers (DHI, LEN) breaking out alongside
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning: BLDR was closed 4 days ago for a +3.18% realized gain on UNDERDONE conf-3 flag with a horizon-exit rationale (10d >= 5d grading window closed). The new flag today re-surfaces BLDR as OVERDONE conf 3 on momentum extension into a worsening mortgage backdrop (rates at 9-month high, refi demand down 18%). To re-buy, two conditions: (1) confidence >= 4 (this flag is conf 3, fails bar), and (2) genuinely new information. The new flag reframes BLDR as OVERDONE rather than UNDERDONE, citing rate headwinds and momentum weakness—this is a directional inversion, not new information about the company. The macro backdrop (rising rates, softening housing demand) is not novel versus the exit; it has merely confirmed the thesis that rate elevation and housing weakness are asserting themselves. The flag explicitly calls this a 'recurring fade setup' without a fresh catalyst URL or company-specific event. Return WATCH. If a material capital return (buyback expansion, dividend increase) or a rate reversal occurs, reassess at higher confidence.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with SMH/AVGO semi rally; no 8-K filed
entries.thesis: ONTO ripping alongside SMH +4% and AVGO +4.7% with no company-specific 8-K. Move magnitude is roughly 2x the sector ETF, which is consistent with higher-beta semi-cap equipment names that typically amplify SMH moves. Nothing on the tape suggests this is overdone or underdone — it's a beta trade.
entries.what_kills: An ONTO-specific catalyst (analyst upgrade with raised PT, contract win, M&A chatter) emerging post-close — would reclassify as UNDERDONE if magnitude exceeded news

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.thesis: AEIS up sharply but five-day still negative, suggesting today is a recovery within a downtrend driven by the broader semi-cap rally (SMH +4%). Magnitude reasonable given the bounce-back math and beta. No catalyst signals.
entries.what_kills: AEIS-specific announcement (guidance reset, customer concentration disclosure) emerging — would force reclassification

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with XBI biotech selloff; no 8-K
entries.thesis: PTCT down hard but five-day is positive and volume is BELOW average (0.75x). XBI -4.4% explains roughly half the move, leaving a residual that could be idiosyncratic or simply beta-amplified. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst, no headline visible. Cannot articulate either a bull (oversold bounce) or bear (specific catalyst) case at conf 3+ without knowing what dragged XBI.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.thesis: BEAM -7% on a day XBI is -4.4% is roughly the expected beta for a small-cap pre-revenue gene editing name. Volume is below average (0.87x), arguing against panic selling. Magnitude appears to match sector pressure rather than name-specific bad news.
entries.what_kills: An overnight 8-K on a pipeline setback or partnership termination would reclassify as UNDERDONE

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly met coal price action or peer sympathy
entries.thesis: Met coal name extending a strong multi-day rally on no company-specific catalyst visible to us. Could be coking coal price strength, China stimulus chatter, or short squeeze dynamics — none of which I can verify from the data provided. Volume only roughly average, which argues against a fundamental catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Peers flat or down while AMR runs alone, suggesting idiosyncratic technical action

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma
entries.thesis: BBIO filed an 8.01 + 9.01 on 5/27 (likely a clinical or commercial update on Attruby/acoramidis) and has drifted down on >2.5x volume into a soft XBI tape (-2.25%). The volume signature suggests material news but the modest price reaction is ambiguous — could be a digestion phase before a larger move either direction. Without reading the 8.01 exhibit, I can't tell if it's a script update (likely market-moving) or routine.
entries.what_kills: Exhibit is administrative (e.g., conference presentation slides), making this just XBI sympathy

entries.name: Watsco, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possible HVAC/housing demand concern or peer-driven rotation
entries.thesis: WSO down ~4% in a tape where SPY is +0.55% and with no 8-K — but XLU -1.1% and rate-sensitive/housing-exposed names appear to be the losers today. Could be tied to housing/HVAC demand concerns or a sell-side note we can't see. Without a visible catalyst, can't articulate either bull (oversold/buy-the-dip) or bear (fundamental break) at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Confirms it's just sector rotation and snaps back tomorrow

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.catalyst: Defense sector bid on Iran missile headlines
entries.thesis: Up ~14% on 2.5x volume, five-day +17% — strong but less extended than AVAV. Kratos has genuine ongoing CCA/drone program newsflow and the Iran tape provides a coherent reason. Magnitude is sharp but not yet at the extension level where a fade is high-conviction. Watch for whether this is following AVAV's setup or has its own contract catalyst.
entries.what_kills: If five-day pushes past 25%+ without contract news, fade setup becomes much more attractive

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No identified company catalyst; possibly oil-down/airline-up macro beta
entries.thesis: Stock is up sharply over five days with another leg today and no company-specific catalyst visible to us. Could be fuel cost tailwind from oil's collapse (Iran de-escalation), broader airline re-rating, or unreported analyst action. Without evidence I can't pick a direction at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Peer airlines flat or down while ALK rips alone strongly suggests unknown idiosyncratic news; if oil rebounds and ALK holds, the fuel narrative breaks

entries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
entries.catalyst: 5/21 earnings 8-K, continuation move on risk-on tape
entries.thesis: DECK printed earnings via 8-K on 5/21 and has continued higher into today on above-average volume. Without the actual print details (guidance, HOKA/UGG splits) I cannot tell if the continuation is justified follow-through on a beat or short-covering overshoot in a quality compounder that had been beaten down. Both bull and bear cases require the segment data I don't have.
entries.what_kills: Either side requires the actual report contents to commit.
